methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $500M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
Collaborative Robotics is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Brad Porter, currently valued at $500M+ post-money on $150M+ total raised. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of five same-sector, same-stage private companies (1X Technologies, Bedrock Robotics, Dexterity, Fairmat, and GrayMatter Robotics) alongside one public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a typical 1.9x multiplier to the $500M last post-money valuation, implying $950M. Peer-median analysis across the five private comparables yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B—reflecting meaningful dispersion within the sector at this stage. The peer-median method carries weight given the homogeneity of stage and sector alignment.

Synthesizing both approaches produces a triangulated range of $808M (low) to $1.9B (high), with a median of $1.3B. This range brackets the current $500M valuation, suggesting modest upside through the next funding event. Key uncertainties include the wide peer dispersion (driven by 1X Technologies' $10.0B outlier valuation) and the limited liquidity benchmark from public markets. The analysis supports a valuation in the $1.3B–$1.6B band absent material operational changes or market repricing of frontier robotics valuations.
